overview: From 2026-06-23 to 2026-06-24, elizaos/eliza had 137 new PRs (114 merged), 0 new issues, and 9 active contributors.

topPRs.title: feat: add @s7g-network/plugin-s7g — DePIN settlement layer for Eliza agents
topPRs.author: Sovereign7G
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Adds the Sovereign 7G Network plugin (@s7g-network/plugin-s7g) to the ElizaOS monorepo — a DePIN settlement layer for AI agents.

## Features

- **8 actions:** GET_LIQUIDITY, GET_YIELD, ARBITRAGE, RISK, EXECUTE, HL_ARBITRAGE, HL_LIQUIDITY, HL_YIELD
- **DeFi Operations:** Liquidity management, yield optimization, arbitrage
- **Hyperliquid Integration:** Perpetual futures, funding rate arbitrage
- **DePIN Settlement:** Cross-chain USDC via CCTP, x402 micropayments
- **Risk Assessment:** Stablecoin de-peg detection, liquidation monitoring

## Architecture

The plugin communicates with 29 autonomous S7G agents via ACP protocol at api.7g.sol. All actions are registered through the standard ElizaOS Plugin interface.

## Files

- plugins/plugin-s7g/ (4 files: package.json, tsconfig.json, index.ts, src/index.ts)
- Follows ElizaOS monorepo conventions (ESM, bun, workspace peer deps)

topPRs.title: refactor(registry): move first-party registry out of app-core to @elizaos/registry/first-party, plugin-owned entries
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
Moves the first-party curated registry out of `app-core` into **`@elizaos/registry/first-party`** and flips entry ownership to **plugin-side** with build-time aggregation. (#9177, the game-app removal this was stacked on, is now **merged**; this PR is auto-retargeted to `develop`.) Three commits:

### 1 — extract the package + shim + runtime overlay
- Moved `schema.ts` / `loader.ts` / `index.ts` / `app-registry.ts` out of `packages/app-core/src/registry/` into `packages/registry/src/first-party/`, exported as **`@elizaos/registry/first-party`**.
- `packages/app-core/src/registry/index.ts` is now a **one-line re-export shim**, so `@elizaos/app-core/registry` + the app-core barrel stay stable — the sole cross-package consumer (`plugin-registry`), the capacitor-bridge tsconfig path, and the hyperliquid vitest alias need **no change**.
- Added **`registerRegistryEntry()`** — a Symbol-keyed global overlay so any plugin can contribute/override an entry **at runtime** (validated fail-loud; runtime wins by `id`).
- Preserved the boot-critical invariants verbatim (`var cacheSlot` TDZ-hardening, circular-import self-heal, on-device `process.argv[1]` fallback). Moved the 3 registry tests with the code.

### 2 — plugin-owned entries + aggregation
- Relocated all **110 entries**: 77 → their owning plugin/package dir as `registry-entry.json`; 33 → `first-party/curated/` (8 built-in app-viewers, 23 entries for non-vendored plugins, 2 no-npmName). Deleted the central `entries/` dir.
- Added **`generate.ts`** — scans `plugins/*` + `packages/*` `registry-entry.json` (object or array) + `curated/`, validates fail-loud, dedupes by `id`, writes a single committed **`generated.json`**. Scripts: `generate:first-party` + `generate:first-party:check` (CI drift gate).
- Loader now reads the **single `generated.json`** (vs scanning per-kind dirs) → on-device staging is one file, mirroring the community registry pattern.

### 3 — plugins ship their entry on publish
- Added `registry-entry.json` to the `files` array of all 77 plugins/packages that own one, so a published plugin carries its own registry metadata for downstream aggregation (the extensible, plugin-side path).

### Verification
- `loadRegistry()` output **BYTE-IDENTICAL** to the pre-refactor golden — after the package move *and* the plugin-side migration: **110 entries** (18 app / 68 plugin / 24 connector), identical ids/npmNames/groups.
- Plugin-side overlay proven (register → 110→111; override-by-id keeps 111 with the new value); shim resolves end-to-end.
- Typecheck clean (`@elizaos/registry`, `app-core`, `plugin-registry`); registry tests green (23); `generated.json` drift-check green; smartglasses gate self-test green.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

topPRs.title: Feat/plugin trust layer for AI Agents
topPRs.author: stvor-hq
topPRs.body:
# Relates to

New plugin. Opens discussion around ERC-8183 as a lifecycle standard for agent-to-agent commerce in elizaOS.

# Sync with develop

- [x] Rebased onto latest `origin/develop`
- [x] `bun run verify` passes

Within `packages/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc`:

$ bun run verify

✓ TypeScript type-check passes
✓ Vitest suite passes

35 tests passed.

# Risks

Medium.

This PR adds a new opt-in plugin under `packages/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc`. No existing runtime behavior is modified.

The plugin registers:

- 1 Service (`agent_commerce`)
- 4 Actions
- 1 Provider

State is persisted through the existing `runtime.createMemory()` API using the `erc8183:job:` namespace.

Known operational risks:

- hydration currently loads up to 1000 job records per agent
- rate limiting is process-local and not distributed
- reputation persistence is memory-backed by default

# Background

## What does this PR do?

Adds an ERC-8183-inspired commerce workflow for elizaOS agents.

The plugin enables one agent to:

1. Create a job
2. Fund a job
3. Submit a deliverable
4. Evaluate the result
5. Complete, refund, expire, or abort the workflow

State machine:

OPEN → FUNDED → SUBMITTED → COMPLETE
                           ↘ REFUND
              ↘ EXPIRED
         ↘ ABORTED

## Registered extension points

| Type | Name |
|--------|--------|
| Service | agent_commerce |
| Action | CREATE_SECURE_JOB |
| Action | FUND_SECURE_JOB |
| Action | SUBMIT_DELIVERABLE |
| Action | JOB_STATUS |
| Provider | COMMERCE_CONTEXT |

## Persistence

Jobs are stored through `runtime.createMemory()` using:

erc8183:job:<jobId>

Each state transition appends a new memory record.

On restart, the store lazily hydrates from memory records and reconstructs the latest job state using the highest `updatedAt` value.

## Security features

- SHA-256 payload attestation
- Deliverable hash verification
- Reputation-based funding gate
- Rate limiting
- State transition validation
- Idempotent terminal transitions

## What kind of change is this?

Feature (non-breaking).

All changes are additive.

# Documentation changes needed?

No.

The package includes its own README.

# Testing

## Where should a reviewer start?

- `src/elizaos/index.ts`
- `src/service.ts`
- `src/state-machine.ts`
- `src/store/elizaos.ts`

## Detailed testing steps

Run:

```bash
cd packages/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc

bun run verify

Expected result:

35 tests passed
0 failures

Optional lifecycle demo:

LOG_LEVEL=info bun run demo-lifecycle.ts

The demo exercises:

- createJob
- fundJob
- submitJob
- evaluateJob
- abort path
- persistence path

Evidence
Real LLM-call trajectory
N/A.

The plugin currently exposes deterministic actions and state-machine logic. No scenario-runner integration exists yet.

Backend + frontend logs

Verification output:

RUN  v4.1.5

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       35 passed (35)

Exit code: 0

Lifecycle demo output includes:

Created job -> OPEN
Funded job -> FUNDED
Submitted deliverable -> SUBMITTED
Evaluated job -> COMPLETE
Hash mismatch -> ABORTED

Screenshots (before / after) + video walkthrough
N/A — backend plugin, no UI.

Audio / voice walkthrough
N/A.

Known limitations
Hydration loads at most 1000 memory records per agent.
Rate limiting is process-local.
MemoryReputationProvider is not persistent across restarts.
Distributed deployments require a custom reputation backend and shared rate-limit implementation.

topPRs.title: feat(plugin): add security layer between agents
topPRs.author: stvor-hq
topPRs.body:
# Relates to
MEV bot Jaredfromsubway.eth was drained $7.5M (Jun 21, 2026) via attacker-controlled contracts tricking automated financial execution logic , the same attack class that threatens AI agents making autonomous financial decisions. This plugin addresses that surface with cryptographic counterparty verification and integrity checks before funds release.

# Sync with develop
- [x] Rebased/merged onto the latest origin/develop; zero conflicts
- [ ] `bun run verify` — `@elizaos/prompts#build` fails due to Node.js v20 
      incompatibility in the monorepo (unrelated to this plugin). 
      Plugin itself builds successfully: `@elizaos/plugin-agent-commerce:build ✅`

# Risks
Low. New standalone plugin, no changes to existing packages. 
Opt-in only — agents load it explicitly.

# Background
## What does this PR do?
Adds `@elizaos/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc` - a security layer for financial 
transactions between ElizaOS agents using cryptography primitives.

Implements ERC-8183 job lifecycle (create → fund → submit → evaluate) with:
- ML-KEM-768 payload hashing (PayloadHasher with timingSafeEqual)
- LLM prompt injection detection (SecurityGuard)
- SHA-256 deliverable integrity verification
- Role-based access control for job evaluation
- Rate limiting for API calls

## What kind of change is this?
Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)

# Documentation changes needed?
My changes do not require a change to the project documentation.
README included in the plugin package.

# Testing
## Where should a reviewer start?
`packages/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc/src/index.ts` — plugin entry point
`packages/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc/src/state-machine.ts` — core logic

## Detailed testing steps
1. Add plugin to agent character file:
   `plugins: ["@elizaos/plugin-agent-commerce-pqc"]`
2. Start agent: `bun run dev`
3. Send: "Create job for agent-bob: build REST API, budget 100"
4. Verify job created with state OPEN
5. Send: "Submit deliverable for job-xxx: API deployed at https://..."
6. Verify deliverable hash stored, state → SUBMITTED

# Evidence
## Real LLM-call trajectory
N/A — plugin is additive, no model/prompt changes

## Backend + frontend logs
N/A — standalone plugin, no backend changes

## Screenshots (before / after) + video walkthrough
N/A — no UI changes

## Audio / voice walkthrough
N/A

## Discord username
sapog_kun

topPRs.title: [codex] fix deterministic response-handler tool calls
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary
- allow response-handler evaluators to attach deterministic tool calls to the Stage-1 plan
- execute deterministic response-handler tool calls through the normal action executor before invoking the planner
- make the direct private-chat fast path yield when a response-handler evaluator claims the turn, so explicit view/app commands can run VIEWS instead of only replying with an acknowledgement

## Root Cause
Live desktop/Cerebras view-switching was producing replies like "opening your Inbox now" but staying on /chat. Trajectories showed the runtime was taking the direct private-chat fast path and executing zero tools, bypassing the app-control response-handler evaluator that should force VIEWS.

## Validation
- bun run --cwd packages/core test src/runtime/__tests__/response-handler-evaluators.test.ts src/__tests__/message-runtime-stage1.test.ts --coverage.enabled=false
- bun run --cwd packages/core typecheck
- bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-app-control typecheck
- bun run --cwd plugins/plugin-app-control test src/evaluators/view-command-shortcut.test.ts --coverage.enabled=false
- git diff --check
- live desktop/Cerebras UI smoke on rebased develop: 6/6 passed for check my messages, muéstrame mi calendario, revisa mi correo, 我的待办事项, カレンダーを開いて, 지갑 열어

completedItems.body:
